title: helianthemum crassifolium subsp. glaucum
intro: Helianthemum crassifolium subsp. glaucum, also known as Rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a succulent shrub that is native to the Mediterranean region and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Helianthemum crassifolium subsp. glaucum include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, digestive problems, and respiratory issues.
